source.title: Section 321 Repeal Takes Effect - ALS Industry Insights

extractions.claim: De minimis exemption for China/HK eliminated May 2, 2025 with 54% ad valorem duty. Full worldwide suspension effective August 29, 2025.
extractions.evidence: May 2, 2025: de minimis exemption eliminated for products of China and Hong Kong, packages face 54% ad valorem duty. July 30, 2025: Executive order suspending de minimis treatment for ALL countries effective August 29, 2025. Permanent statutory repeal under One Big Beautiful Bill Act takes effect July 1, 2027.

verification.verifier_notes: CBP Trade Information Notice and Congressional records confirm timeline

extractions.claim: De minimis NOT restored by SCOTUS ruling. Remains dead. Executive Order affirmed de minimis termination survives.
extractions.evidence: The President's Executive Order affirmed that the termination of duty-free de minimis treatment for all countries remains in effect. De minimis was suspended by separate executive action, not IEEPA.

verification.verifier_notes: Holland & Knight and multiple legal analyses confirm de minimis unaffected by SCOTUS

extractions.claim: Scale of impact: ~4 million parcels/day entered US under Section 321, ~1.36 billion/year. Temu and Shein accounted for ~600,000 daily shipments.
extractions.evidence: Approximately 4 million parcels entered the United States each day under Section 321, adding up to about 1.36 billion packages annually. The volume jumped from 220 million in 2016 to over a billion, with Chinese e-commerce platforms like Shein and Temu accounting for roughly 600,000 daily shipments alone.

verification.verifier_notes: CBP and Congressional Research Service data

extractions.claim: Post-de minimis, postal shipments face $80-$200 per package duty. From Feb 28, 2026 only ad valorem methodology available.
extractions.evidence: Postal shipments are subject to new duty rates of $80-$200 per package based on country tariff levels. The specific duty methodology option will cease to be available six months after August 29. From February 28, 2026, forward, only the ad valorem duty methodology may be used.
